scope: Critical review of the Ilumionate codebase focusing on design, structure, architecture, data flow, concurrency, and compiler-confirmed risks.

build_verification.command: xcodebuild -project Ilumionate.xcodeproj -scheme Ilumionate -sdk iphonesimulator -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
notes: The build succeeded after rerunning outside the sandbox.
The target is compiled with default MainActor isolation and multiple upcoming concurrency features enabled.
Several concurrency warnings are already present and would become harder failures under stricter Swift 6 migration.

issues.title: Project-wide MainActor default isolation is masking architecture boundaries instead of defining them
issues.problem: The app target defaults almost everything to MainActor while still compiling in Swift 5 mode. That lets non-UI services, analyzers, and helper types inherit UI isolation accidentally, which is why unrelated types like ProsodyAnalyzer and TechniqueDetector are already generating actor-isolation warnings.
issues.impact: This increases main-thread coupling, hides true ownership boundaries, and creates a brittle migration path to stricter concurrency settings.
issues.recommendation: Remove MainActor as the default isolation for the whole target, then explicitly annotate only UI-facing types and APIs. Treat services, analyzers, and pure data-processing types as nonisolated or actor-owned by design.

issues.title: Root view owns global singletons as @State, creating hidden dependencies and unclear ownership
issues.problem: ContentView mixes locally owned state with process-wide singleton state using the same property wrapper. That makes dependency boundaries implicit and makes the root view responsible for app services, playback state, and analysis state all at once.
issues.impact: Testing becomes harder, previews become less representative, and state ownership is ambiguous because the same view owns both ephemeral UI state and global app state.
issues.recommendation: Move app-scoped services into explicit dependency injection at the app/root container level. Use environment injection or a dedicated app model for shared state and keep @State limited to view-owned ephemeral values.

issues.title: Audio file state is snapshotted once in ContentView while analysis writes back through UserDefaults side effects
issues.problem: ContentView loads the audio file list once on appear, while AnalysisStateManager mutates the persisted list later via UserDefaults. The in-memory array passed into HomeView is not refreshed when analysis results are persisted.
issues.impact: Views can render stale analysis/transcription state until the root view is recreated. This creates confusing UI behavior and a non-obvious source of truth.
issues.recommendation: Replace the UserDefaults bridge with a single observable store for audio files. Persist through that store, not around it.

issues.title: AnalysisStateManager mutates persisted AudioFile storage behind the UI's back
issues.problem: The analysis subsystem reaches across layers and directly edits serialized UI/library state in UserDefaults. That couples background processing, persistence, and presentation concerns into one manager.
issues.impact: Any future change to AudioFile persistence or library state can break analysis completion. It also guarantees hidden side effects and stale in-memory views.
issues.recommendation: Introduce a repository or store that owns AudioFile mutation and persistence. AnalysisStateManager should emit results, not rewrite serialized UI state itself.

issues.title: MainActor-isolated service protocols force expensive pipeline stages onto the UI isolation domain
issues.problem: The protocols that define transcription, content analysis, and session generation are all MainActor-isolated. That makes the pipeline architecture UI-centric by contract, even though these responsibilities are CPU- and I/O-heavy background work.
issues.impact: This unnecessarily serializes work through MainActor, spreads actor-isolation warnings into pure analysis code, and makes later refactoring more difficult.
issues.recommendation: Remove MainActor from non-UI protocols. Keep progress publishing isolated separately from the work execution path.

issues.title: AnalysisPipeline is advertised as parallel but still centers heavy work on MainActor
issues.problem: The coordinator itself is MainActor-isolated, and it depends on MainActor-isolated service protocols. The only explicit background hop is prosody extraction via Task.detached, so the broader pipeline is not actually actor-separated from UI state.
issues.impact: The design invites UI jank, accidental actor-hopping, and misleading assumptions about where computation runs.
issues.recommendation: Make the pipeline itself non-UI. Publish progress back to MainActor only at the edges.

issues.title: Detached prosody work breaks structured cancellation and leaves cleanup to manual bookkeeping
issues.problem: Task.detached breaks task hierarchy and cancellation inheritance. If AI analysis fails or the caller is cancelled before awaiting the detached task, prosody work can continue longer than intended.
issues.impact: This wastes work and makes the pipeline harder to reason about under cancellation or errors.
issues.recommendation: Use `async let` or `withThrowingTaskGroup` so prosody and AI analysis stay in the same structured task tree.

issues.title: Session generation claims to avoid MainActor but explicitly hops back to MainActor
issues.problem: The comment and implementation directly contradict each other. The expensive generation stage is explicitly run on MainActor.
issues.impact: Long generation work can block UI responsiveness and also reinforces the broader architectural mistake of treating analysis/generation as UI work.
issues.recommendation: Move generation into a nonisolated service or a dedicated actor. Only publish completion back on MainActor.

issues.title: Streaming analysis path is still placeholder code and returns nondeterministic fake characteristics
issues.problem: The production code path produces random audio characteristics instead of analyzing the data. The extension below also returns a fixed AnalysisResult without using the downloaded audio data.
issues.impact: Two analyses of the same streaming track can disagree, generated sessions are not reproducible, and user trust in the feature is undermined.
issues.recommendation: Either gate this feature behind an unfinished flag or replace the placeholder path with deterministic analysis before exposing it to users.

issues.title: Streaming AI extension ignores input data and returns a hard-coded result
issues.problem: The API surface claims to perform AI-based streaming analysis but the implementation never reads `data` and returns a canned response.
issues.impact: This is misleading at both the code and product level, and it prevents downstream behavior from matching the actual track content.
issues.recommendation: Rename this path as a fallback heuristic if that is the intent, or connect it to a real analysis pipeline that consumes the audio sample.

issues.title: StreamingManager throws away the generated analysis and stores a fabricated replacement on AudioFile
issues.problem: The manager returns a generated session based on one analysis path, then persists a different hard-coded analysis onto the AudioFile.
issues.impact: The persisted metadata can disagree with the generated session and any later UI or regeneration step will operate on inconsistent state.
issues.recommendation: Return the actual analysis alongside the session and persist that exact value. Do not synthesize a second analysis object.

issues.title: SoundCloud client credentials and tokens are stored in UserDefaults
issues.snippet:
soundCloudClientId = UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: "SoundCloud_ClientId") ?? ""
soundCloudSecret = UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: "SoundCloud_Secret") ?? ""
...
UserDefaults.standard.set(soundCloudClientId, forKey: "SoundCloud_ClientId")
UserDefaults.standard.set(soundCloudSecret, forKey: "SoundCloud_Secret")
...
UserDefaults.standard.removeObject(forKey: "SoundCloud_AccessToken")
issues.problem: API client secrets and tokens are persisted in plain app preferences storage rather than a secure credential store.
issues.impact: This weakens protection for third-party credentials and makes accidental disclosure or insecure backup restoration more likely.
issues.recommendation: Move secrets and tokens to Keychain. Only keep non-sensitive UI preferences in UserDefaults.

issues.title: AudioFile has side-effectful persistence behavior hidden in a computed property
issues.problem: A domain model extension is performing hidden persistence I/O via property access. Reading or writing `audioFile.streamingTrack` is not a pure model operation.
issues.impact: This makes the model harder to reason about, complicates tests, and can lead to silent storage growth without a clear lifecycle.
issues.recommendation: Move streaming track persistence into a repository/store layer. Keep AudioFile as a value model with explicit serialization boundaries.

issues.title: ClosedRange retroactive Codable conformance is ignored because Swift already provides it
issues.problem: The build emits warnings that this conformance conflicts with the one in the Swift standard library and will be ignored.
issues.impact: This adds noise to every build and suggests the code depends on serialization behavior it does not actually own.
issues.recommendation: Delete this extension and rely on the standard library conformance.

issues.title: ProsodyAnalyzer is documented as background-safe but currently leaks MainActor isolation into nonisolated code
issues.snippet: //  All methods are nonisolated and safe to run on a background thread.
issues.problem: The compiler already warns that ProsodyAnalyzer's nonisolated code is calling MainActor-isolated APIs and referencing main-actor-isolated state. The documentation promise is not true in the current target configuration.
issues.impact: This code will become increasingly fragile under stricter concurrency checking and undermines the intended background execution model.
issues.recommendation: Make the analyzer and its dependencies fully nonisolated, or move the truly UI-isolated dependencies out of this code path.

issues.title: TechniqueDetector initializer pulls config through a MainActor-isolated loader from a nonisolated context
issues.problem: The build warns that this nonisolated initializer calls a MainActor-isolated method. This is another symptom of app-wide actor isolation leaking into pure analysis code.
issues.impact: The detector is not as pure or concurrency-safe as intended, and future strict mode could turn this into a compile error.
issues.recommendation: Make configuration loading nonisolated and inject config explicitly into analysis code.

issues.title: AudioLightSyncPlayer timer closure captures self in concurrently executing code
issues.problem: The compiler warns that this captured `self` pattern is unsafe in concurrently executing code and will be an error in Swift 6 mode.
issues.impact: This is a migration blocker and indicates the timer/update path is not modeled with the right actor or lifetime guarantees.
issues.recommendation: Capture a strong local binding before entering the Task or move timer updates into a dedicated MainActor method without crossing concurrency domains inside the closure.
